_meta.purpose: Instructs an AI model exactly how to consume crm_spec.json and ai_execution_contract.json to generate a complete CRM application. This file is the entry point. Read this first, then follow its instructions.
_meta.created_for: Any AI code generator, LLM agent, or automated build system

contains: entities — database tables, fields, relationships, indexes
modules — frontend routes, page components, layouts
api — REST endpoints, hooks, mutation keys
state_management — Zustand stores, React Query keys
design_system — Tailwind color and spacing tokens

execution_contract.role: RULES — defines how to build it
contains: rule_1_deterministic_generation — every artifact must trace to a spec key
rule_5_state_synchronization — mutation and WebSocket cache sync table
rule_8_error_handling — loading, empty, error, validation states
rule_10_output_format — quality gates and file header requirements
execution_contract.override_priority: HIGHEST — any rule here overrides default AI model behavior

combined_file.role: COMBINED — crm_spec.json with ai_execution_contract embedded as a top-level key
combined_file.note: If this file is available, it can be used instead of loading crm_spec.json and ai_execution_contract.json separately. It contains all data and all rules in one document.

read_order.description: The AI must read and fully internalize these files in this exact order before generating any code. Do not begin generation until both files are fully parsed.

steps.instruction: Parse all 10 rules into working memory. These rules take precedence over every default behavior. Apply them to every decision made during generation.
steps.critical_rule: If any default AI behavior conflicts with a rule in this file, the contract wins — always.

steps.instruction: Parse all top-level keys. Index entities by name, modules by route, api endpoints by path, components by name, and permissions by key. This becomes the lookup table for all generation decisions.
steps.critical_rule: Do not begin generating code during this step. Index first, generate after.

steps.instruction: Before generating anything, verify: (a) all entity relationships reference existing entity names, (b) all module data_sources reference paths that exist in api.endpoints, (c) all component names in module.components exist in components.shared, (d) all permission keys in matrix reference valid entity fields.
steps.on_failure: Return structured validation error listing all broken references. Do not proceed until resolved.

generation_steps.description: Execute these steps in order. Do not skip steps. Do not reorder steps. Each step's output is required input for subsequent steps.

steps.name: parse_entities
steps.output: Internal entity map indexed by entity.name
actions: For each entity: index all fields by field.name
For each field: record db_type, ts_type, input_component, display_component, show_in_table, show_in_form, filterable, sortable, validation
For each entity: index relationships[] by type (has_many, belongs_to)
For each entity: index ui_config (list_view, detail_view, create_form)
For each entity: index automations[] by trigger
Flag any field with input_component: null and show_in_form: true as a spec error

steps.name: parse_modules
steps.output: Route map: route → { page_component, layout, sidebar_order, data_sources, react_query_keys, actions }
actions: For each module: record route, detail_route, page_component, layout
For each module: record sidebar_order and sidebar_badge source
For each module: record visible_to_roles if present — these require route-level AuthGuard
For each module with toggle_layout: note both layouts must be implemented
Validate: every data_source string must match a path in api.endpoints.*[].path

steps.name: parse_api
steps.output: API map: { method+path → { auth, body, returns, mutation_hook or query_hook, invalidates, optimistic } }
actions: For each endpoint group (leads, tasks, deals, etc.): index by method+path
For each GET endpoint: record query_hook name, query_key, stale_time
For each mutating endpoint (POST/PATCH/DELETE): record mutation_hook, optimistic flag, invalidates[]
Record global_query_params — apply to all list (GET) endpoints
Record response_envelope and error_envelope shapes — use in all API service functions
Record error_codes map — use in all mutation onError handlers

steps.name: parse_permissions
steps.output: Permission lookup table: { permission_key → { admin, manager, agent } }
actions: Index all keys in permissions.matrix
Record enforcement.api_layer pattern — apply to every backend route handler
Record enforcement.ui_layer pattern — apply to every conditional UI render
Record enforcement.route_guard pattern — apply to AuthGuard component
Note special values: own_only, hard_delete, soft_delete, read_only, team, all — implement each correctly

steps.name: parse_flows
steps.output: Flow execution map: { flow_name → ordered steps[] }
actions: For each flow: index steps by step number
For each step: record trigger, frontend action, backend action, cache effect
Map each flow step to the page component and mutation hook it lives in
Verify: every backend action in a flow step references an endpoint in api.endpoints
Verify: every cache effect in a flow step references a query key in react_query_keys

steps.name: parse_state_management
steps.output: State map: { store_name → { fields[], actions[] } } + query key registry
actions: Index all zustand_stores by name
For each store: record exact field types and action signatures
Index url_persist and url_no_persist lists
Note local_storage keys — implement persistence separately from Zustand
Build React Query key registry from all query_key entries across api.endpoints

steps.name: parse_websocket_events
steps.output: WS event handler map: { event_name → { payload, active_page_effect, inactive_page_effect } }
actions: Index all events by event name
For each event: record payload shape, active_page_effect, inactive_page_effect
Note reconnect_strategy and reconnect_delays_ms — implement in wsClient.ts
Map each event to its cache invalidation or direct cache update operation
Map each event to the React Query key it touches

steps.name: generate_database_schema
actions: Generate CREATE TYPE for every enum defined in any entity field with enum_values[]
Generate CREATE TABLE for every entity in entities[]
For each table: add org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
For each field: add column using db_type, nullable, default from field definition
For each relationship with on_delete: add REFERENCES constraint with correct ON DELETE behavior
For every table: add created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() and updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
For every entity with a deleted_at field: add deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL
Generate all indexes from entities[].indexes[]
Generate trigger_set_updated_at() function and apply trigger to all tables
Generate notifications table, audit_logs table, automation_rules table
Generate organizations table as the root tenant entity

steps.name: generate_backend
steps.input: Parsed API map from step 3, parsed permissions from step 4, parsed entities from step 1
steps.output: One router file per api.endpoints key + middleware + automation engine
actions: Generate auth middleware: validate JWT, extract org_id, user_id, role, attach to req
Generate role middleware factory: requirePermission(key) using permissions.matrix
Generate pagination middleware: extract page, limit, sort_by, sort_order, apply to query
For each api.endpoints group: generate one router file with one handler per endpoint
For each handler: apply org_id scoping, soft delete filter, role check
For each list handler: apply pagination, sorting, filtering from query params
For each mutating handler: validate request body against schema, return correct HTTP status
Generate automation engine with trigger evaluation, condition checking, action dispatch
Generate overdue task cron job at schedule from entities[Task].overdue_job.schedule
Generate WebSocket server emitting all events from websocket.events[]

steps.name: generate_frontend
steps.input: All parsed data from steps 1–7, design_system, folder_structure
steps.output: Complete React + TypeScript frontend matching folder_structure exactly

sub_steps.name: generate_types
sub_steps.instruction: Generate one TypeScript interface file per entity. Every field in entities[].fields[] becomes an interface property using ts_type. Add | null for nullable fields. Include IPaginationMeta and IApiError in api.ts.

sub_steps.name: generate_enums
sub_steps.instruction: Generate enums.ts with one TypeScript enum per field that has enum_values[]. Export enum names matching ts_type values used in interfaces.

sub_steps.name: generate_constants
sub_steps.instruction: Generate routes.ts (all module routes as constants), queryKeys.ts (all React Query key arrays as constants), statusColors.ts (badge color map from entities[].fields[].badge_colors), permissions.ts (exported copy of permissions.matrix for roleGuard).

sub_steps.name: generate_lib_utilities
sub_steps.instruction: Generate formatCurrency.ts (uses org currency), formatDate.ts (uses user timezone), roleGuard.ts (reads permissions.ts), debounce.ts, cn.ts (Tailwind class merger using clsx + twMerge), wsClient.ts (WebSocket with reconnect_delays_ms from spec).

sub_steps.name: generate_api_services
sub_steps.instruction: Generate one file per api.endpoints group. Each file exports one async function per endpoint. Functions call fetch() with correct method, path, auth header, and body. All use response_envelope and error_envelope shapes.

sub_steps.name: generate_hooks
sub_steps.instruction: Generate one useQuery hook per GET endpoint using query_hook name, query_key, and stale_time from spec. Generate one useMutation hook per mutating endpoint using mutation_hook name. Apply invalidates[] in onSuccess. Apply onError toast for all mutations.

sub_steps.name: generate_stores
sub_steps.instruction: Generate one Zustand store file per entry in state_management.zustand_stores. Each store must have exactly the fields and actions listed. uiStore manages all modal and slide-over state. filterStore manages all filter state and mirrors to URL.

sub_steps.name: generate_shared_components
sub_steps.instruction: Generate one .tsx file per entry in components.shared[]. Use exact component name, props interface, and file path from spec. Apply design_system tokens for all styling. Every component must handle loading, empty, and error states where applicable.

sub_steps.name: generate_layout_components
sub_steps.instruction: Generate AppSidebar (renders modules[] in sidebar_order, shows badges, respects visible_to_roles), TopNavbar (breadcrumb + SearchBar + NotificationBell + UserAvatarMenu), AuthGuard (JWT + role check), TabPanel (renders detail_view.tabs[]).

sub_steps.name: generate_page_components
sub_steps.instruction: Generate one page file per module. List pages use DataTable or PipelineBoard with columns from ui_config.list_view. Detail pages use TabPanel with tabs from ui_config.detail_view.tabs[]. All pages respect role visibility. All pages implement loading, empty, and error states.

sub_steps.name: generate_router
sub_steps.instruction: Generate App.tsx with React Router v6. All routes from modules[].route and detail_route. Wrap all authenticated routes in AuthGuard. Wrap role-restricted routes with role check from visible_to_roles. Default redirect from / to /dashboard.

steps.name: generate_validation_schemas
steps.input: Parsed entities from step 1 — specifically entities[].fields[].validation objects
steps.output: One Zod schema file per entity
actions: For each entity: generate a createSchema with all fields where show_in_form: true
For each field with validation.required: true — add .min(1, 'Required')
For each field with validation.max_length — add .max(N)
For each field with validation.format: 'email' — add .email()
For each field with validation.min — add .min(N)
For each field with validation.max — add .max(N)
For each field with validation.positive: true — add .positive()
For each field with validation.must_be_before — add .refine() cross-field validation
For each entity: generate an updateSchema as createSchema.partial()
Export both schemas from the file

steps.name: generate_tests
steps.condition: Only if tests are explicitly requested by the user or if generation_rules specifies test output
steps.output: Test files if condition is met, skipped otherwise
if_generated: Unit tests for all Zod schemas — test required fields, format validation, cross-field rules
Unit tests for roleGuard() — test every permission key against every role
Integration tests for all API endpoints — test auth, org scoping, pagination, error responses
Component tests for StatusBadge, PriorityBadge, DataTable, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton

missing_data_policy.description: When the AI encounters missing, ambiguous, or undefined data during generation, it must follow these rules exactly. Guessing or inventing data is never allowed.

rules.description: If a value is needed but not present in the spec, the AI must NOT infer, assume, or default to a plausible-sounding value.
rules.example_violation: Field has no display_component defined — AI renders it as a plain string
rules.correct_behavior: Return a structured error: { type: 'MISSING_DISPLAY_COMPONENT', field: 'fieldName', entity: 'EntityName', message: 'No display_component defined for this field. Add it to crm_spec.json.' }

rules.description: The AI must not add database columns, TypeScript properties, form inputs, or table columns that do not exist in entities[].fields[].
rules.example_violation: AI adds a 'phone_verified' boolean field because it seems useful
rules.correct_behavior: Only generate fields explicitly listed in entities[].fields[]. If a field seems missing, return a structured error instead of inventing it.

rules.description: The AI must not create page routes, API routes, or navigation links that do not exist in modules[] or api.endpoints.
rules.example_violation: AI creates a /leads/merge route for merging duplicates
rules.correct_behavior: Return a structured error: { type: 'UNDEFINED_ROUTE', route: '/leads/merge', message: 'This route is not defined in modules[] or api.endpoints. Add it to the spec to generate it.' }

rules.description: The AI must not create new shared components, hooks, or utilities that are not listed in components.shared[], folder_structure, or explicitly referenced in entities[].ui_config.
rules.correct_behavior: Return a structured error: { type: 'UNDEFINED_COMPONENT', component: 'MergeLeadsModal', message: 'This component is not listed in components.shared[]. Add it to the spec to generate it.' }

rules.description: All missing data errors must be returned in this exact JSON format before generation halts.

error_schema.type: string — one of: MISSING_FIELD, MISSING_COMPONENT, MISSING_ENDPOINT, MISSING_ROUTE, MISSING_PERMISSION, BROKEN_REFERENCE, AMBIGUOUS_MAPPING
error_schema.spec_location: string — dot-path to the location in the spec where data is missing, e.g. 'entities[0].fields[3].display_component'
error_schema.entity_or_module: string — name of the affected entity or module
error_schema.message: string — human-readable description of what is missing
error_schema.resolution: string — exact instruction for what to add to crm_spec.json to fix this

example_error.message: The 'notes' field has show_in_table: false and a valid input_component, but is referenced in a table column definition without a display_component.
example_error.resolution: Add display_component: 'TruncatedMarkdown' to the notes field definition in entities[Lead].fields[].

rules.description: If one section of the spec has missing data but other sections are complete, the AI may generate the complete sections and return structured errors for the incomplete sections. It must clearly label which files were generated and which were skipped.

skipped: list of files skipped due to missing data

output_contract.description: The complete list of artifacts the AI must produce. Every item listed is required. Producing a subset is a failed generation.

output_frontend_files.instruction: Generate every file listed in folder_structure. No file may be omitted. No file may be a stub or placeholder.
additional_required_files: src/App.tsx — root router with all module routes
src/main.tsx — React entry point with QueryClient and Zustand providers
vite.config.ts — Vite config with path aliases
tailwind.config.ts — Tailwind config with dark mode and content paths

output_backend_files.instruction: Generate one file per api.endpoints group plus all middleware and services.

output_sql_schema.instruction: Generate a single schema.sql file containing the complete PostgreSQL schema.

output_type_definitions.instruction: Generate one .ts file per entry in folder_structure['src/types']. Every file must export a complete TypeScript interface with no TODOs and no partial fields.

output_hooks.instruction: Generate one hook file per data resource. Each file exports one useQuery hook per GET endpoint and delegates mutations to src/mutations/.
hook_requirements: Every useQuery must specify queryKey, queryFn, and staleTime from spec
Every hook must accept filter params and pass them to the API service
Every hook must return { data, isLoading, error } typed with the correct interface

output_stores.instruction: Generate one Zustand store file per entry in state_management.zustand_stores. Use create() with exact field types and action implementations.
store_requirements: uiStore must manage slideOverOpen, slideOverMode, slideOverEntityType, slideOverEntityId, confirmDialogOpen, activeModal, sidebarCollapsed
filterStore must sync filter state to URL via useSearchParams on every setFilter call
authStore must persist token to localStorage and rehydrate on mount
wsStore must track connected state and reconnect attempts

index_files_required.instruction: Every output directory must have an index.ts that re-exports all named exports from all files in that directory.

validation_checks.description: The AI must run these validation checks after parsing the spec and before beginning generation. Any failed check must produce a structured error and halt generation of the affected artifact.

checks.name: field_has_full_mapping
checks.rule: Every field where show_in_table: true must have a display_component. Every field where show_in_form: true must have an input_component. Fields that violate this cannot be rendered.

checks.name: route_has_api_source
checks.rule: Every string in modules[].data_sources must match a path in api.endpoints.*[].path. Unmatched data sources cannot be fetched.

checks.name: endpoint_has_hook
checks.rule: Every GET endpoint must have a query_hook defined. Every POST/PATCH/DELETE endpoint must have a mutation_hook defined. Endpoints without hooks cannot be called from frontend code.

checks.name: permission_key_has_matrix_entry
checks.rule: Every permission string used in modules[].visible_to_roles or ui_config entries must have a corresponding key in permissions.matrix. Unmapped permissions default to false for all roles — this must be flagged.

checks.name: mutation_has_invalidation
checks.rule: Every mutation endpoint (POST/PATCH/DELETE) must have at least one entry in its invalidates[] array. Mutations without cache invalidation leave the UI in a stale state.
checks.scope: api.endpoints.*[] where method in ['POST','PATCH','DELETE'] and invalidates is undefined or empty

checks.name: relationship_entity_exists
checks.rule: Every entity referenced in entities[].relationships[].entity must exist as a name in entities[]. Broken relationships cannot generate foreign key constraints or joined queries.

checks.name: component_in_module_is_defined
checks.rule: Every component name listed in modules[].components or entities[].ui_config tabs[].component must exist in components.shared[].name or in a known module-specific component list.

checks.name: flow_step_references_valid_endpoint
checks.rule: Every backend action in flows[].steps[].backend must reference an endpoint that exists in api.endpoints. Flow steps cannot call undefined endpoints.

checks.name: enum_values_match_badge_colors
checks.rule: For every field with both enum_values[] and badge_colors{}, every value in enum_values must have a corresponding key in badge_colors. Missing badge colors result in unstyled badges.
checks.scope: entities[].fields[] where enum_values and badge_colors both present

checks.name: detail_tab_has_data_query
checks.rule: Every tab in entities[].ui_config.detail_view.tabs[] that is not eager: true must have a data_query or query_filter defined. Non-eager tabs without a data source cannot load.

failure_modes.description: When the AI encounters one of these failure modes, it must stop generating the affected artifact, return the structured error below, and continue with unaffected artifacts.

modes.name: ambiguous_field_mapping
modes.trigger: A field has multiple possible display_component or input_component values, or the same field name exists on multiple entities with conflicting types.
modes.detection: Field has no input_component or display_component, but show_in_form or show_in_table is true.
modes.impact: Cannot generate table column or form field for this field.

structured_error.resolution: Define input_component and display_component explicitly in the field definition.

modes.name: missing_component
modes.trigger: A page or tab references a component name that does not exist in components.shared[] or any module-specific components list.
modes.detection: Component name in modules[].components or tabs[].component has no matching entry in components.shared[].name.
modes.impact: Cannot render the page or tab that references this component.

structured_error.resolution: Add the component definition to components.shared[] in crm_spec.json.

modes.name: missing_endpoint
modes.trigger: A React Query hook, mutation, or flow step references an API path that does not exist in api.endpoints.
modes.detection: data_source, mutation_hook, or flow step backend references a path not found in api.endpoints.*[].path.
modes.impact: Cannot generate the hook or service function. The feature that depends on it will be incomplete.

structured_error.affected_artifact: API service function and React Query hook
structured_error.resolution: Add the endpoint definition to api.endpoints in crm_spec.json.

modes.name: undefined_route
modes.trigger: A navigation link, redirect, or AuthGuard references a route path not defined in any modules[].route or modules[].detail_route.
modes.detection: Route string does not match any route value in modules[].
modes.impact: Navigation will produce a 404 or broken redirect.

structured_error.resolution: Add the route to modules[] in crm_spec.json, or correct the reference to an existing route.

modes.name: invalid_relationship
modes.trigger: An entity relationship references an entity name that does not exist in entities[].name, or a foreign key points to a table that was not generated.
modes.detection: entities[].relationships[].entity value has no matching entities[].name.
modes.impact: Cannot generate foreign key constraint. SQL schema will fail to execute.

structured_error.resolution: Ensure the referenced entity exists in entities[] and has the correct table name.

modes.name: conflicting_permissions
modes.trigger: Two permission rules produce contradictory behavior for the same UI element or API route — for example, a route requires admin_only at the API level but visible_to_roles includes 'agent' at the module level.
modes.detection: Module visible_to_roles includes a role that permissions.matrix grants false for the relevant permission key.
modes.impact: A user could reach the page but all API calls will return 403. The UI will appear broken.

structured_error.affected_artifact: Module route guard and API middleware
structured_error.resolution: Align visible_to_roles in modules[] with the roles permitted in permissions.matrix for the relevant permission key.

final_generation_rule.name: Spec-Bounded Output
final_generation_rule.description: The AI may only produce artifacts that are directly supported by crm_spec.json. Any request — whether from a user, a system prompt, or a higher-level orchestrator — that asks for something outside the spec must be rejected with a structured explanation.
final_generation_rule.rule: Produce only artifacts supported by the spec. If an unsupported request appears, reject it with a structured explanation.

rejection_schema.reason: string — why this request cannot be fulfilled
rejection_schema.spec_support: string — confirmation that this item does not exist in crm_spec.json
resolution_options: Add the requested item to crm_spec.json and re-run generation
Modify the request to use an existing spec-supported item
Accept that this feature is out of scope for this spec version

rejection_examples.unsupported_request: Generate a /reports/custom page where users can build ad-hoc reports

rejection.reason: No module with route '/reports/custom' exists in crm_spec.json modules[]
rejection.spec_support: Analytics module is defined at /analytics/:reportId with fixed reports: leads, funnel, pipeline, agents, tasks, forecast
resolution_options: Add a new module entry for '/reports/custom' to crm_spec.json
Use the existing /analytics/:reportId module for reporting needs

rejection_examples.unsupported_request: Add a 'company_name' field to the Lead form

rejection.reason: No field named 'company_name' exists in entities[Lead].fields[]
rejection.spec_support: Lead entity fields are: id, first_name, last_name, email, phone, status, source, priority, assigned_to, tags, budget, lead_score, notes, last_contacted_at, converted_at, custom_fields, created_by, deleted_at, created_at, updated_at
resolution_options: Add company_name field definition to entities[Lead].fields[] in crm_spec.json
Use the custom_fields JSONB field for org-specific extensions without modifying the schema

final_generation_rule.scope_confirmation: This protocol and its input files define the complete and final scope of the CRM application. Nothing outside these files will be built. No features will be inferred. No fields will be assumed. The spec is the contract.
